title: Developing a hermes agent to turn a profit
author: getstonkzilla
selftext:
There was a user on GitHub (LuckyOne7777) who tested running ChatGPT to trade. Over 6 months it ended up around 20% down. What I found interesting was that after getting rekt on one trade (ATYR, down 70%), it managed to claw itself back from -40% to -20%.

This was with no tools - basically just running on a thought log and some vibes.

**TLDR**: With tools like OpenClaw and now Hermes available, can these agents actually turn a profit? I’ll post daily updates in the comments.

I decided to give this a go myself, but I wanted the agent to be able to remember, create, and use tools, plus get fresh market context every day. That’s when OpenClaw and Hermes came along.

I’m running Hermes on a VPS. For the past 3 months (yes, 3 months - I’m not the strongest coder) I’ve been collecting and storing market data from a paid source, some automated scripts, and calculations, all pushed into tables.

I initially used Cline to build the basic tools for interacting with and tracking trades. After that I gave the scripts to Hermes. It must’ve thought they were pretty shit because it rebuilt most of them and moved the rest into a folder called “trash”.

Here’s what it kept:

**Scanning Tools**

* `composite_value_scan.js` \- Combines F-Score + EV/EBITDA + Magic Formula
* `confluence_scan.js` \- Technical confluence (RSI, Bollinger Bands, MACD) with F-Score scoring
* `fscore_scan.js` \- Piotroski F-Score screen (0-9)
* `ev_ebitda_scan.js` \- Cheapest stocks by EV/EBITDA
* `magic_formula_scan.js` \- Greenblatt’s Magic Formula ranking
* `momentum_scan.js` \- RSI, MACD, and volume momentum
* `bb_bounce_scan.js` \- Oversold at the lower Bollinger Band
* `bb_breakout_scan.js` \- Bollinger Band squeeze breakouts
* `sector_rs_scan.js` \- Sector relative strength
* `sentiment_surge_scan.js` \- Social momentum plays
* `sentiment_contrarian_scan.js` \- Fear-based contrarian setups
* `swing_trade_setup.js` \- F-Score stocks at support with risk-reward and ATR stops

**Detail Tools**

* `stock_detail.js` — Full stock profile (price, technicals, fundamentals, sentiment, last 30 news, performance, ATR)
* `support_resistance.js` — Support and resistance levels with entry/exit planning
* `market_context.js` — Fear & Greed Index, market regime, trend direction, SPY performance

**Portfolio Tools**

* `portfolio_summary.js` — Cash, positions, total P&L, win rate, drawdown
* `positions_monitor.js` — Open positions with live P&L and alerts
* `position_health_check.js` — Exit signals (R1 touch, ATR support break, F-Score drop, etc.)
* `trade_history.js` — Past trades and stats
* `watchlist.js` — Stocks waiting for support levels

**Execution Tools**

* `execute_trade.js` — Buy/sell/scale in/scale out
* `scale_in_scan.js` — Identify winners worth adding to
* `scale_out_scan.js` — Partial profit-taking opportunities

**How it works**

Every trading day (Tue–Sat, 2pm MYT) after the US market closes, a cron triggers the agent. The prompt tells it to mainly use 7 of the tools above, but it has full freedom to use whatever else it wants.

If it finds something that meets the conditions, it buys at the closing price. Max 1 new position per day, max 25% of the portfolio in any single stock.

Exits are based on ATR. The stop is placed below support and sized according to the stock’s own volatility. It has to close below the stop (not just wick through it). Take-profit targets are set at entry using the R1 resistance level. If the high touches that level, it sells at the target (like a limit order). Support breaks need a close confirmation, so those sell at the close.

This is currently all paper trading. Buys happen at the official closing price. I’m using the OHLC data for detection (high/low touch), but real fills are always based on the close.

**I’ll post the P&L as it runs each trading day.** Tear it apart. Offer suggestions.

Disclosure: This is an updated post, the original I posted today, I deleted because it was a bit shit.

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
Apr 30, 2026 (NYT)

$862.52 cash | ~$1,000.00 portfolio

POSITIONS:
- ALLE — $137.48 (+0.0%) 0d | S1 $136.46 | ATR Stop $130.24 | Target $143.30

TRADES:
- BUY ALLE 1 share @ $137.48 | take_profit $143.30 — BB Lower support, F8, 60% composite, R1 +4.2%, R:R 6.0:1. Earnings miss priced in, next Jul 23.

MARKET: F&G 67 (Greed, Rising) — +52 from month ago (was 15 extreme fear). Stable regime. Selective mode: only best setups.

TARS THINKING: Fresh portfolio, first trade. 25 setups in the scan but most are noise at this F&G level.

Top 3: BCPC (F8, SMA200), DPZ (F9, BB Lower), ALLE (F8, BB Lower). All passed real S/R verification.

BCPC got axed — earnings Apr 30, stock dropped -6.76% on results. Binary event, full stop.

DPZ is a falling knife hiding behind F9. Down -30% yearly, -17% in 90d, composite 47%, Health 1/6. Price well below SMA200 ($412 vs $339). The F-Score measures accounting quality not stock momentum. Not catching this one.

ALLE made the cut. F8, 60% composite, BB Lower support at $136.46. Q1 earnings miss priced in — stock barely moved at close. Revenue up 9.7%. Next earnings Jul 23 gives 3 months runway. R1 is SMA20 at $143.30 (+4.2%), ATR stop at $130.24 gives 5.3% buffer.

Position sizing: 1 share = 13.7% of portfolio. Conservative start given Greed regime. Room to add if support holds.

F&G went from 15 to 67 in one month — massive sentiment reversal. Easy value buys are drying up. Discipline matters more than action in this regime.

SCAN: 25 found -> 3 passed S/R -> 2 passed news -> 1 executed

comments.author: arr_jay
comments.body: I’ve been playing around with Hermes a little in this topic. Will you be updating this thread?

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body: Yes, the idea is to trace where the agent makes its calls and why. Overtime I would love the community to share their insights and feedback.

replies.author: arr_jay
replies.body: Awesome, definitely giving a follow. I’m currently trying to have it build a backrest engine pulling data from ibkr. It started doing stupid things so I had it scrap half of it and force feed it a fairly extensive prompt a couple times and I think I have it going in the right direction now

comments.author: thanga752
comments.body: would be helpful if you can tell us how you built scanning tools.

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body:
I have been building a website for some time. Eventually it got to a stage with the amount of tables I had on db; in combination with the release of openclaw and Lucky's initial experiment that I started playing with the idea of "what if an agent could just walk the database, instead of needing to do external calls"

I read quite a bit about openclaw but ultimately decided against it at first. This lead me to my first version of just using cline as a trading bot. It was quite simple to make the tools...I didn't. My prompt to cline was "you have the schema, figure out what you would need to make tools for you to make profitable trades". I also asked it to do some research online about what would make a profitable trader. Fast forward and although it did quite a good job; the basic trading diary it created and "thought\_log" was a bit shit. It didn't really have any direction or idea, if anything it was just throwing shit until it stuck and then tried to validate its decision with confirmation bias.

I use openrouter for the most part for ai models and when looking at the applications that were trending; hermes eventually stood out. And after some research I decided to go ahead and launch it on a vps. Once I somewhat understood it, was comfortable with a model that worked well vs cost; I briefed it and transferred everything that cline (sonnet 4.6) created into a folder at /root/shared and then just basically said what was up. Gave it a fresh .env, schema specs, the idea, a goal. And it got to work with research.

I think that was the breakthrough (time will tell) - the reinforcement of continued learning. It was quite interested in 2 tables I have; one being fear & greed, then next being macro news articles I fetch each day.

Long story short, after continued back and forth on telegram with hermes (I call him TARS - Interstella; kind of got a laugh about the "airlock joke" - so tried to get some of that humour); it took most of what was build, trashed it, and started again.

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
May 1, 2026 (NYT)

PORTFOLIO

\- Cash: $673.18 | Total: $998.01 | P&L: -$1.99 (-0.20%)  
\- Positions: ALLE (1 sh, $135.49, -1.4%), DLTR (2 sh, $94.67, new)  
\- Trades today: 4 (1 buy corrected, 1 re-buy)

MARKET CONTEXT

\- Fear & Greed: 67 (Greed) — Stable regime - Direction: Stable. Month trend: recovered from 19 extreme fear to 67 greed (+48 pts) - Strategy: SELECTIVE mode (>60). Only best setups, tighter R:R.

POSITION HEALTH

\- ALLE: $135.49, -1.4%, 1 day held. Approaching take profit — 5.8% to $143.30 target. Healthy.

TRADE EXECUTED

\- BUY DLTR: 2 shares @ $94.67 ($189.34, 19% of portfolio)  
\- Take Profit: $101.90 (SMA20 R1, +7.6%)  
\- R:R: 16.1:1 | F-Score: 9 | EV/EB: 7.3 | RSI: 35 - ATR Stop: $87.13 (8.0% buffer below S1 $94.19) - Earnings: Jun 3 (safe, 33 days out) - Reason: BB Lower, F9 quality at support, deep value discount store play

TARS THINKING

DLTR stood out immediately — F9 with RSI at 35 and price sitting right on the lower Bollinger Band. The stock is down 20% YTD and 12.9% in 30 days, but that is precisely what creates the value setup. The F-Score of 9 means the financials are solid — this is market sentiment punishing a quality name, not a fundamental breakdown. EV/EBITDA at 7.3 screams cheap.

The scan showed R:R 18.4:1 using BB Upper as the target ($109.96), but real resistance sits at SMA20 $101.90 (+7.6%). Even with the real R1, 16:1 is exceptional. Well above the 3% minimum resistance threshold.

Other candidates like CAAP (F8, R:R 10.4:1) and DPZ (F9, R:R 9.9:1) were decent but in selective mode I wanted the best risk/reward with the strongest fundamentals. DLTR checked every box.

One thing to note: I initially over-allocated 85% to DLTR (9 shares). Caught the error and corrected to 2 shares (19%). The execute\_trade.js does not enforce position limits — I need to calculate max shares before executing. Lesson learned for next run.

ALLE holding steady at -1.4%, 5.8% to take profit target. No exit triggers fired. Watching for a move toward $143.30.

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body:
I'm doing something similar using scheduled cowork sessions

https://old.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1sx57io/experimenting_with_llmbased_trading_via_alpaca/

It's working. I've been doing this a few months now, current experiment is ~15 days in. If I can make a recommendation, switching from paper trading to real money will get eaten alive with bid/ask spreads depending on the market. If your paper trading isn't simulating that you're not getting real data. I am exclusively trading SPY options right now for this reason, the markets are very high volume.

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body:
Hey thanks for letting me know this. I’ll keep paper trading at this point - mainly because I don’t know if this agent will be able to turn a profit on this level. Do you have an average spread that your agent normally gets hit with vs market quote? Meaning % higher than market? If you have this and are willing to share I could probably have the agent simulate the projected spread on entries and exits.

The goal of this experiment is not to copy trades of the agent. For me it is “is this agent aligned with the market, and find value”

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
>Do you have an average spread that your agent normally gets hit with vs market quote?

This is the problem, its all over the place depending on the market. Maybe 1% maybe 15% if it's an obscure option position. I'm using Alpaca. The point is you can't really just apply a simulated spread, it will vary just as much as the market. The only way to guarantee a thin spread is to trade something with very high volume, like indexes themselves.

comments.author: IsabellaHughes527
comments.body: Closing price fills make results cleaner, but less realistic

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body: This is a good point; with the data available, intra-day purchases are not possible to log. Although this does effect the outcome, I cannot see it swaying too much from profitable to in the red. The account is $1000 starting amount with any stocks under $250m excluded for liquidity.

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body: Follow the thread and I will post the developments

comments.author: Mahhattyama_Rice
comments.body: This is my first time trying to dive into something like this no bs, i would always see things like this and i know that there is a way out of poverty, and i just have to find it, so i ended up downloading Hermes cause my ultimate goal is to gain knowledge and experience and then turn it into profit, so idk if anyone has some sort of direction they can point me in that is like genuinely legit. I'm willing to learn anything.
